paper_map.title: Microstructure and mechanical properties of Fe–30Mn–9Al–C–3Ni low-density steel manufactured by selective laser melting
paper_map.abstract_summary: This study presents the first fabrication of Fe–30Mn–9Al–C–3Ni low-density steel via selective laser melting (SLM) using plasma rotating electrode process (PREP) powder. The effect of laser power (70–150 W) on microstructure and mechanical properties is systematically investigated. The sample printed at 110 W achieves the best combination of high yield strength (887.1 MPa), ultimate tensile strength (1076.8 MPa), elongation (40.80%), and hardness (294 HV). The results demonstrate the potential of SLM for producing complex-shaped low-density steels with outstanding mechanical properties.
paper_map.research_objective: To investigate the influence of laser power on the microstructure and mechanical properties of Fe–30Mn–9Al–C–3Ni low-density steel fabricated by selective laser melting for the first time, and to achieve optimized comprehensive mechanical performance.
main_process_variables: laser scanning strategy: 67° rotation per layer
key_sections: 1. Introduction: reviews background of Fe-Mn-Al-C steels, advantages of SLM, and states research gap and objectives
2. Materials and methodology: describes PREP powder, SLM printing parameters, sample naming, and characterization methods for microstructure and mechanical testing
3. Results and discussion: 3.1 Powder characterization, 3.2 Alloy microstructure characterization, 3.3 Mechanical properties (tensile, hardness) and fracture analysis
4. Conclusion: summarizes key findings, including optimal laser power and properties
